ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general tea beverage class; no specific entry for Teh C Siu Dai (evaporated milk tea, less sugar, Singaporean hawker style). Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers prepared tea beverages broadly; no specific code for Singaporean evaporated milk tea with reduced sugar. Closest defensible code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept found for Teh C Siu Dai. SNOMED CT does not enumerate hawker-style regional tea variants.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard hawker-stall cup of Teh C Siu Dai is approximately 250 ml, equivalent to 250 g, and is typically consumed as a breakfast or mid-morning beverage.

nutrient_source.title: Teh C Siu Dai — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Foodo for Teh C Siu Dai (cr_id D09020069). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record with confidence 0.95. Detailed fatty acid fractions (monounsaturated, polyunsaturated, trans fat) and micronutrients not reported in the HPB record are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Teh C Siu Dai in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 55 (low) by analogy with sweetened milk tea and tea-with-milk beverages reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008). Glycemic load per 250 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (55 × 9.925) / 100 ≈ 5.5. The reduced sugar content ('siu dai') lowers the GL relative to standard Teh C. Note: A GI of 55 is considered low, not medium, per University of Sydney GI Database guidelines (low < 55, medium 56-69, high > 70).

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar in a sweetened beverage; low dietary fibre; sodium 24 mg/100 g
hypertension.guidance: Teh C Siu Dai contains added sugar (3.74 g/100 g) and qualifies as a sweetened beverage under hypertension dietary guidelines; individuals with hypertension should request Teh C Kosong (no sugar) to eliminate the sugar load while retaining the potassium and antioxidant benefits of black tea.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with added sugar (3.74 g/100 g); liquid sugar causes rapid postprandial glucose rise
type2_diabetes.guidance: Teh C Siu Dai is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage and is not recommended for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; the 'siu dai' (less sugar) modification does not eliminate the SSB concern, and patients should substitute with Teh C Kosong or unsweetened black tea to avoid postprandial glucose spikes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Added sugar and saturated fat from evaporated milk; black tea polyphenols provide partial cardioprotective benefit
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The added sugar (3.74 g/100 g) and saturated fat from evaporated milk contribute to cardiovascular risk when consumed regularly; however, the black tea polyphenols (theaflavins, thearubigins) offer partial cardioprotective benefit, and individuals with cardiovascular disease should request the unsweetened version (Teh C Kosong) to maximise the benefit-to-risk ratio.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar promotes pro-inflammatory mediators; black tea polyphenols are anti-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The added sugar in Teh C Siu Dai promotes inflammatory mediators relevant to rheumatoid arthritis; ordering Teh C Kosong (no sugar) allows patients to benefit from the anti-inflammatory polyphenols in black tea without the pro-inflammatory sugar load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Teh C Siu Dai is a traditional Singaporean hawker-stall beverage consisting of strong brewed black tea blended with evaporated milk and a reduced quantity of sugar. The name derives from Hokkien: 'Teh' (tea), 'C' (evaporated milk, as opposed to 'O' for black or 'tarik' for pulled), and 'siu dai' (少甜, meaning less sweet). It is a variant of the standard Teh C, which uses a full measure of sugar; the 'siu dai' modifier signals to the hawker to halve or reduce the sugar added. The beverage is served hot or iced and is a staple of Singapore's kopi tiam (coffee shop) culture, commonly consumed at breakfast or as a mid-morning refreshment. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 37 kcal, 1.24 g protein, 1.77 g fat, 3.97 g carbohydrate (of which 3.74 g is sugar), 0.99 g dietary fibre, and 24 mg sodium. The evaporated milk contributes saturated fat and calcium, while the black tea provides polyphenolic antioxidants (theaflavins and thearubigins) with established cardiovascular and anti-inflammatory properties. Despite the reduced sugar, Teh C Siu Dai is still classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) under ADA and HPB guidelines, making it unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or hypertension who require strict sugar restriction. The glycemic index is estimated at 55 (low) by analogy with sweetened milk tea, with a glycemic load of approximately 5.5 per 250 ml serving. Individuals managing diabetes, hypertension, or rheumatoid arthritis are advised to order Teh C Kosong (no sugar) instead.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Foodo Singapore database (cr_id D09020069, confidence 0.95) with exact match. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for sweetened milk tea; this reduces GI confidence to medium for that field only, but overall record confidence remains high due to the exact HPB source match for all macros.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions and micronutrient data for Teh C Siu Dai are not reported in the HPB Foodo database (cr_id D09020069) as of 2026-04-16. 2. saturated_fat_g estimated at 1.10 g/100 g based on the known fat composition of evaporated milk (approximately 62% of total fat as saturated fat per USDA FoodData Central entry for evaporated milk, NDB 01086); this is an adaptation and is documented accordingly. 3. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for sweetened milk tea; no direct GI measurement for Teh C Siu Dai exists in any approved source. 4. The input record listed 'keto' as a dietary tag; this has been removed as Teh C Siu Dai contains 3.97 g carbohydrate/100 g and added sugar, which is inconsistent with ketogenic dietary criteria. 5. The input health_suitability used condition key 'diabetes'; this has been updated to 'type2_diabetes' per Veritas naming rules. 6. Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' as GI 55 falls within the low GI range (<55) per University of Sydney GI Database guidelines.
